meta.section: Endpoint Management — Windows (Phase 6 of 7: Experience / browsers / privacy / telemetry)
meta.source: Microsoft Learn (learn.microsoft.com/intune + sharepoint OneDrive policies + deployedge) — portal paths and Settings Catalog categories grounded via the Microsoft Learn MCP on 2026-07-03.
generalNotes: All 13 are Windows Settings Catalog configuration profiles: Devices > Configuration > Create > New policy > Windows 10 and later > Settings catalog. Add the category's settings, then assign to the Windows device group.

The 'U' vs 'D' suffix reflects scope: U = user-scoped settings (apply per signed-in user), D = device-scoped. Some settings only exist in one scope — pick the matching one in the settings picker. Browsers have both a 'Security' profile (protection / enterprise controls) and an 'Experience' profile (homepage, search, UX); keep them as separate profiles as the master splits them.
Most are LOW/invisible; the MEDIUM ones (Edge experience, OneDrive Known Folder Move, block Microsoft accounts) are user-visible — communicate before rollout.

requiredSettings.label: Profile type
requiredSettings.value: Settings catalog — Microsoft Edge (browser UX)

requiredSettings.label: Intent
requiredSettings.value: Homepage / startup pages, default search engine, sync/sign-in behavior, extension controls per baseline

steps: Devices > Configuration > Create > Windows 10 and later > Settings catalog.
Add the Microsoft Edge experience settings (startup/home, default search, sync, allowed extensions); assign to the Windows device group.
Note (MEDIUM impact): changes the visible browser experience (home page / search / sign-in) — brief users. Keep separate from the Edge Security profile below.

requiredSettings.label: Profile type
requiredSettings.value: Settings catalog — OneDrive

requiredSettings.label: Silently move Windows known folders to OneDrive (KFM)
requiredSettings.value: Enabled for Desktop/Documents/Pictures, with your Tenant ID

requiredSettings.label: Silently sign in users to OneDrive (SilentAccountConfig)
requiredSettings.value: Enabled

requiredSettings.label: Files On-Demand
requiredSettings.value: Enabled

steps: Devices > Configuration > Create > Windows 10 and later > Settings catalog.
Add: 'Silently move Windows known folders to OneDrive' (Desktop/Documents/Pictures, tenant ID); 'Silently sign in users to the OneDrive sync app'; 'Use OneDrive Files On-Demand'.
Assign to the Windows device group. (Stage the silent KFM rollout — Microsoft recommends ≤1,000 devices/day.)
Note (MEDIUM impact): Desktop/Documents/Pictures are redirected into OneDrive silently — users see their known folders now backed up; communicate so it isn't mistaken for data moving/loss.

requiredSettings.label: Profile type
requiredSettings.value: Settings catalog (Accounts / Local Policies Security Options)

requiredSettings.label: Block Microsoft (consumer) accounts
requiredSettings.value: Users can't add or log on with personal Microsoft accounts on the managed device

steps: Devices > Configuration > Create > Windows 10 and later > Settings catalog.
Add the 'Accounts: Block Microsoft accounts' / Allow Microsoft Account Connection = blocked setting; assign to the Windows device group.
Note (MEDIUM impact): users can no longer sign in to apps/store with a personal MSA on company devices — expected for a managed fleet, but flag it for anyone who used a personal account.

requiredSettings.label: Profile type
requiredSettings.value: Settings catalog — Microsoft Office (user experience)

requiredSettings.label: Intent
requiredSettings.value: Office app user settings — default file formats, feature toggles, first-run/telemetry prompts (macro security is primarily covered by the ASR + Office security baseline)

steps: Devices > Configuration > Create > Windows 10 and later > Settings catalog.
Add the Microsoft Office user-experience settings per the baseline; assign to the Windows device group.
Note: LOW impact — cosmetic/behavioral Office defaults.

requiredSettings.label: Profile type
requiredSettings.value: Settings catalog — Microsoft Edge (security / enterprise)

requiredSettings.label: Intent
requiredSettings.value: SmartScreen enabled, minimum TLS 1.2, block third-party cookies, control password saving / sync, Application Bound Encryption per baseline

steps: Devices > Configuration > Create > Windows 10 and later > Settings catalog.
Add the Edge security settings (SmartScreen, TLS 1.2 minimum, cookie/popup/notification controls, sync/password-save restrictions); assign to the Windows device group.
Note: LOW impact — enterprise browser hardening; kept separate from the Edge experience profile above.

requiredSettings.label: Profile type
requiredSettings.value: Settings catalog — Google Chrome (requires the Chrome ADMX / Chrome installed)

requiredSettings.label: Intent
requiredSettings.value: Chrome security controls — SafeBrowsing, block third-party cookies, restrict extensions, password manager per baseline

steps: Devices > Configuration > Create > Windows 10 and later > Settings catalog.
Add the Google Chrome security settings (Safe Browsing, extension allow/block, cookie controls); assign to the Windows device group.
Note: LOW impact — only affects devices where Chrome is installed.

requiredSettings.label: Profile type
requiredSettings.value: Settings catalog — Google Chrome (experience)

requiredSettings.label: Intent
requiredSettings.value: Chrome UX at device level — homepage/startup, default search, sign-in/sync behavior

steps: Devices > Configuration > Create > Windows 10 and later > Settings catalog.
Add the Chrome experience settings per the baseline; assign to the Windows device group.
Note: LOW impact — device-level Chrome UX; only where Chrome is installed.

requiredSettings.label: Profile type
requiredSettings.value: Settings catalog — Delivery Optimization

requiredSettings.label: Download mode
requiredSettings.value: HTTP + peering behind same NAT (1) or Group (2) per baseline — controls whether update content is shared P2P across the LAN

steps: Devices > Configuration > Create > Windows 10 and later > Settings catalog.
Set the Delivery Optimization Download Mode (and optional group/bandwidth limits) per the baseline; assign to the Windows device group.
Note: LOW impact — reduces WAN bandwidth by sharing update content between local devices; invisible to users.

requiredSettings.label: Profile type
requiredSettings.value: Settings catalog — Privacy

requiredSettings.label: Intent
requiredSettings.value: Limit consumer data flows — advertising ID off, tailored experiences off, location/app-permission and activity-history restrictions per baseline

steps: Devices > Configuration > Create > Windows 10 and later > Settings catalog.
Apply the privacy settings (disable advertising ID, tailored experiences, activity feed, restrict app permissions) per the baseline; assign to the Windows device group.
Note: LOW impact — reduces consumer telemetry/personalization; pairs with the Telemetry profile.

requiredSettings.label: Profile type
requiredSettings.value: Settings catalog — Experience / Cloud Content

requiredSettings.label: Windows Spotlight
requiredSettings.value: Turn off Windows Spotlight features (dynamic lock-screen images, tips, suggestions, consumer content)

steps: Devices > Configuration > Create > Windows 10 and later > Settings catalog.
Turn off Windows Spotlight / cloud consumer content and suggestions; assign to the Windows device group.
Note: LOW impact — removes lock-screen ads/tips and third-party suggested content.

requiredSettings.label: Profile type
requiredSettings.value: Settings catalog — Personalization / Control Panel Display

requiredSettings.label: Intent
requiredSettings.value: Optionally lock desktop wallpaper/theme/colors so devices keep a consistent look; restrict user changes per baseline

steps: Devices > Configuration > Create > Windows 10 and later > Settings catalog.
Apply the personalization restrictions (e.g. enforce/lock wallpaper or theme) per the baseline; assign to the Windows device group.
Note: LOW impact — users may be unable to change wallpaper/theme if locked; keep light unless the org wants uniform branding.

requiredSettings.label: Profile type
requiredSettings.value: Settings catalog — OneDrive (device-scoped)

requiredSettings.label: Intent
requiredSettings.value: Restrict sync to your tenant (AllowTenantList), block personal OneDrive sync (DisablePersonalSync), sync-app update ring, low-disk-space guards

steps: Devices > Configuration > Create > Windows 10 and later > Settings catalog.
Add the device-scoped OneDrive controls: allow sync only for your tenant ID, block personal OneDrive accounts, set the sync-app update ring; assign to the Windows device group.
Note (MEDIUM impact): personal-OneDrive sync is blocked and only your tenant can sync — expected for managed devices; complements the user-scoped KFM profile above.

requiredSettings.label: Profile type
requiredSettings.value: Settings catalog — System / Diagnostic data

requiredSettings.label: Allow Telemetry (diagnostic data level)
requiredSettings.value: Required diagnostic data (or the baseline level) — enough for WUFB reports / MDE, without full optional data

steps: Devices > Configuration > Create > Windows 10 and later > Settings catalog.
Set the diagnostic data level (Allow Telemetry) to the baseline value; assign to the Windows device group.
Note: LOW impact / invisible — but keep it at least 'Required' so WUFB Reports (Phase 4) and update/security reporting have the data they need.
